title: 01 Why This Matters in HCI
thesis: Describing field evolution is a core SciSci challenge. HCI makes it especially hard because of its multiple paradigms, diverse contributions, and contested core concepts.
figureCaption: SciSci conceptual diagram and HCI plurality visualization.
cautionNote: Interest is in structural reorganization, not prediction or hype.

title: 02 Topology: Minimal Background
thesis: Topology reads connectivity and holes. This project reads connectivity change in concept point clouds. Birth edge, loop, and closure are the three key ideas.
figureCaption: Filtration schematic and birth edge highlight.
cautionNote: A loop is not itself a mature topic or causal mechanism.

title: 03 Existing Approaches
thesis: Each existing method has a clear target. This project complements rather than replaces them, focusing on pre-topic boundary states.
cautionNote: We do not claim existing methods fail; we address a different target.

title: 04 Data and Operationalization
thesis: Yearly spaces are built with strict historical cutoffs so validation remains temporally honest. Cross-area link means semantic community boundary, not institutional interdisciplinarity.
figureCaption: Figure 1: Analysis object flow from papers to candidate pairs.
cautionNote: Cross-year comparison concerns yearly states, not one fixed object identity.

title: 05 Detecting Boundary Structures
thesis: The method operates at local (birth edge), meso (persistent loop), and macro (yearly summaries) scales simultaneously.
figureCaption: Figure 2: Pipeline and loop birth mechanics.
cautionNote: Mathematical definitions specify computation, not causality.

title: 06 Evidence
thesis: Observations are organized by scale: micro (representative cases), meso (ranking quality), and macro (yearly structure).
figureCaption: Figures 3-5: Case studies, baseline comparison, and structural tension.
cautionNote: Validation is retrospective and out-of-time, not prospective deployment proof.

title: 07 How to Read the Outputs
thesis: Cross-area means between semantic communities. Birth means boundary formation. Persistence means unresolved boundary. Death means possible resolution.
cautionNote: Case narratives illustrate plausibility, not inevitability.

title: 08 What This Captures / What It Does Not
thesis: Direct outputs, supported interpretations, and non-established claims are explicitly separated.
cautionNote: Do not conflate structural signals with social causation.

title: 09 Implications for HCI and SciSci
thesis: For HCI: reading subarea reorganization. For SciSci: interpretable structural descriptors. Future: expert validation, corpus expansion, aligned embeddings.
cautionNote: Implications are framed as contributions, not universal prescriptions.
